SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol
Interface MediaObject

All Known Subinterfaces:
MediaGroup, MediaMixer, MediaSession, MixerAdapter, NetworkConnection, ResourceContainer, VxmlDialog
public interface MediaObject

A base class for objects that are created by a Factory, can be released, and can be parameterized.

Each MediaObject has a parent, which is its creator: for example, a MediaGroup is child of a MediaSession, which is child of a MsControlFactory.

Each MediaObject has an ID, unique in the scope of its parent (like a file name is unique in the context of its directory).
The ID can be provided by the application, using the MEDIAOBJECT_ID Parameter. If the application does not provide an ID, the implementation will generate one.

Each MediaObject can be designated by an URI, built by prepending the MediaObject ID with the URI of its parent. (This is similar to building a fullpath name with the file name and directory name).
The scheme for URIs is "mscontrol://".

Field Summary
static Parameter MEDIAOBJECT_ID
          A non-mutable ID string, replacing the system-provided ID.
 
Method Summary
 Parameters createParameters()
          Create an empty Parameters map
 java.util.Iterator<MediaObject> getMediaObjects()
          Return an Iterator over all the children of this MediaObject.
<T extends MediaObject>
java.util.Iterator<T>
getMediaObjects(java.lang.Class<T> classObject)
          Similar to getMediaObjects(), with an additional filtering based on the given type.
 Parameters getParameters(Parameter[] params)
          Gets the value of various Parameter's from the media object.
 java.net.URI getURI()
          Return a URI that uniquely identifies this object.
 void release()
          Release the resources associated to this media object.
 void setParameters(Parameters params)
          Set the value of various Parameter's for the media object.
 

Field Detail

MEDIAOBJECT_ID

static final Parameter MEDIAOBJECT_ID
A non-mutable ID string, replacing the system-provided ID.
The value must be a String starting with a letter or a number.
This attribute can be used in the Parameters argument of the factory methods like MediaSession.createMediaGroup(javax.media.mscontrol.Configuration, Parameters).

Method Detail

getURI

java.net.URI getURI()
Return a URI that uniquely identifies this object.
Examples:
mscontrol://16.16.234.47/MediaSession.ms12
mscontrol://16.16.234.47/MediaSession.ms12/MediaGroup.mg73
mscontrol://16.16.234.47/MediaSession.ms12/NetworkConnection.nc33
mscontrol://16.16.234.47/MediaSession.ms12/Mixer.mx45/MixerAdapter.ma3

release

void release()
Release the resources associated to this media object.
The call is cascaded to the children of this object (the objects created by it).

setParameters

void setParameters(Parameters params)
Set the value of various Parameter's for the media object.

For each key in the supplied Parameters map, the corresponding parameter of the media object is set to the value of that key in the map.

If a Parameter refers to a resource that is not present, or the media object has no meaning associated with a particular Parameter, the setting for that Parameter is quietly ignored.

Parameters:
params - a Parameters map in which the keys are Parameter's and the values are assigned to the parameter with that name.

getParameters

Parameters getParameters(Parameter[] params)
Gets the value of various Parameter's from the media object.

Each entry in the returned Parameters map holds the value of the corresponding Parameter. If the argument is null, then get all parameters supported by this object. For example: 

 Parameters params = getParameters(null); // on return, params contains all parameters and their values.
If a key refers to a Resource that is not present, or the resource has no meaning associated with a particular Parameter, the key is ignored, no error is generated, and that key does not appear in the returned Parameters map.

Note: This can be used to know the list of parameters supported by a given object.

Parameters:
params - - an array of Parameter's identifying parameters to be retrieved.

Returns:
a Parameters map of Parameter's and their values.

createParameters

Parameters createParameters()
Create an empty Parameters map

getMediaObjects

java.util.Iterator<MediaObject> getMediaObjects()
Return an Iterator over all the children of this MediaObject.
The children are the MediaObjects created by this object.
The released objects are not included. Note that the objects in the list may be released at any time after the call returns (either because of a call to MediaObject.release() or parentOfMediaObject.release(), or because something happened to cause the object to be terminated by the implementation).
Only the direct children are returned (no recursion).
The returned Iterator will throw UnsupportedOperationException if remove() is called.
If there are no children, the method returns an empty Iterator.

getMediaObjects

<T extends MediaObject> java.util.Iterator<T> getMediaObjects(java.lang.Class<T> classObject)
Similar to getMediaObjects(), with an additional filtering based on the given type.

Example: 

 Iterator myAdapterIterator = myMixer.getMediaObjects(MixerAdapter.class);

Type Parameters:
T - Objects of a different type are filtered out
Parameters:
classObject - The Class object of the desired type
Returns:
An Iterator over all children of type T
